text:
There once was a wise old owl named Ollie who lived high atop an old oak tree. Every day, Ollie would gaze down at the bustling forest below. One late spring evening, he noticed a curious hummingbird struggling with a berry. Ollie swooped down, helping the bird fly off safely.

That night, Ollie reflected on his life. He hadn’t had much luck lately, save for those occasional kind deeds. It made him think about how lucky he was to have loving owls as friends. That night, Ollie decided he’d start a small sanctuary to help birds.

Morning came, and Ollie filled a bowl with sugar water. Birds began to visit, drawn by the sweet nectar. Before long, Ollie had a small community of feathered friends.
